data.narrative:
Infleqtion is a public-stage quantum computing company founded by Dana Anderson and Pranav Gokhale. The target currently trades at $1.8B post $210M in total capital raised. The quantum computing sector includes 3 public comparables (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers, creating a reference set of 11 companies across development stages.

Peer-median analysis applied to four same-sector, same-stage public comparables yields an implied valuation of $3.8B. The peer set spans IonQ at $11.0B, Rigetti at $5.5B, D-Wave at $2.0B, and Archer Materials at $60M, establishing a range of $60M to $11.0B. The median of $3.8B reflects the central tendency across this heterogeneous group. This single triangulation method, given sector volatility and early-stage commercial maturity across quantum computing, represents the primary quantitative anchor available in the data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $3.2B to $4.3B, centered on $3.8B. This implies an upside of 111% from the current $1.8B valuation. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($60M to $11.0B), reflecting different technology approaches and commercialization timelines within quantum computing, and the absence of revenue or profitability metrics to calibrate stage-specific multiples. A single-method triangulation limits confidence; incorporation of private comparables or venture-return approaches would strengthen conviction.
